message:
I've been thinking about how much easier it is to handle emergencies when you've actually talked through the basics with your household first. Ready.gov has a straightforward planning guide that walks you through making a simple family plan—nothing complicated, just the practical stuff like meeting places and contact info. It's one of those things that takes an afternoon but genuinely makes a difference.

What's one thing your family already talks about when it comes to emergencies—or is this something you'd like to start?
